name: Dheeren Tejani
bio: Specializing in Generative AI, Custom LLMs, and High-Performance Optimization
title: AI Engineer

projects.title: RangeFlow
projects.description: An AI research project library aims to implement interval arithmetic in Deep Learning, enabling Continual Learning, Enhancing Model Robustness against adversarial attacks, and providing Uncertainty Estimation. Right now, it is in its early stages of development, but is showing promising results.

projects.title: Custom GPT-2 LLM (Built from Scratch)
projects.description: Engineered a 124M-parameter Transformer model entirely from first principles. Implemented Causal Self-Attention and trained on the C4 dataset using Mixed Precision (AMP) and 8-bit optimizers to master the internal mathematics of Large Language Models.

projects.title: Latent Diffusion Model (Stable Diffusion from Scratch)
projects.description: Built a complete Text-to-Image generation pipeline implementing the math behind Stable Diffusion. Architected a VAE for latent compression, a U-Net with Cross-Attention for text conditioning, and manual DDPM/DDIM noise schedulers.

projects.title: Diabetic Retinopathy Diagnosis
projects.description: A Deep learning Classification + NLP model that detects Diabetic Retinopathy disease and classifies them into 5 classes + provide the lesions present in them and gives Natural Language diagnosis

projects.title: Cyberpunk Theme Image Generation
projects.description: An AI Image Generation application that generates high quality, multiple aspect ratios images from user prompts, it uses fine-tuned stable-diffusion 1.5 model

projects.title: Movie Recommendation System
projects.description: A content-based movie recommender that analyzes movie metadata to compute similarity scores and deliver intelligent suggestions. Built with Python, the system includes a Jupyter notebook for data processing and model generation, and a Streamlit app for an interactive user experience.

workExperience.company: Vivekanand Education Society of Arts, Science and Commerce (Autonomous)
summaryPoints: Developed the official Exam Remuneration System for both written and practical examinations (Serverless).
Digitalizing workflows for 100+ faculty, replacing manual paper processes with a centralized, secure online platform.

Built an end-to-end PDF parsing pipeline, replacing a 70B LLaMA-based system with a 4-bit quantized 7B Qwen model, achieving 10x parameter reduction and cutting inference costs by nearly 7x (2.67 USD/hr on H100 → 0.40 USD/hr on L4).
Developed a FastAPI backend with async job queuing and encryption, handling the full pipeline from document preprocessing to structured output postprocessing.
